judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit (a), (b), and (c) strongly, but Report 2 additionally identifies the shared RESULTS_DB collision risk (d) that Report 1 dismisses as dead code, making Report 2 the more complete answer.

report_1.evidence: Group 3 — `pipeline/registry.py` (11 lines): F-02 ... F-05 ... F-09 ... Three independent one-line edits in an 11-line file — cheap enough to be one engineer's single small commit rather than three people touching the same tiny file.

report_2.evidence: Batch B — Registry config (`pipeline/registry.py`): F-02, F-05, F-09. ... I'd keep all three on **one** engineer rather than split by finding — the file is small enough that splitting three people onto it just creates avoidable diff/merge overhead for no parallelism benefit.

report_1.evidence: Wave 1 (parallel start): Groups 1-4 all start immediately afterward — none of them depend on each other. ... Group 2, F-01 before F-11 ... [Group 3 registry] cheap enough to be one engineer's single small commit

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 (fully parallel, start immediately): Batches A, B, C, D, and the verify-only half of E (F-08). All five touch disjoint files ... [Batch C] F-01 + F-11 together ... [Batch B registry] keep all three on one engineer

report_1.evidence: F-06 — needs a number, not just direction, before it's assignable. ... This is a scoring-policy decision (it changes every future score's partial-credit boundary) rather than a mechanical bug fix ... Get explicit target thresholds from whoever owns rubric policy first.

report_2.evidence: F-06 — don't assign as "tighten the thresholds." ... This changes the actual scores the pipeline assigns to real saved model outputs ... Escalate... don't guess." Whoever owns rubric/grading policy needs to supply target threshold values before this becomes code.

`tests/conftest.py:4` defines `RESULTS_DB` defaulting to a single fixed path, `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`, overridable only via an env var. ... any engineer whose fix adds a test that exercises scoring persistence ... needs to set their own `RESULTS_DB` — otherwise concurrent local test runs on the same machine could read/write the same file.
